title: Justice for 9/11 Act
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
plain_summary: This bill is about three men who are accused of planning the September 11th terrorist attacks. It says that even if these men made deals with prosecutors to avoid the death penalty, they can still be put on trial and could face the death penalty. If any of these men are found guilty and sentenced, they must stay in prison at Guantanamo Bay in Cuba. They would have to be kept alone in their cells with very limited contact with other people and could never be moved to a different prison.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Justice for Nine Eleven Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Trial and Post-Trial Matters Relating to Certain Individuals Detained at Guantanamo
sections.text: This section covers the trials and punishment of three specific men held at the Guantanamo Bay prison. These men are Khalid Shaikh Mohammad, Walid Muhammad Salih Mubarak Bin Attash, and Mustafa Ahmed Adam al Hawsawi. They are accused of being involved in the September eleventh, two thousand one terrorist attacks. Even if these three men made deals with prosecutors where they agreed to plead guilty to some crimes, those deals cannot stop the government from putting them on trial for the September eleventh attacks. The government can still try them in military courts or regular courts for those attacks. When these three men go to trial for the September eleventh attacks, judges will be allowed to sentence them to death if they are found guilty. This is true no matter what other laws might say. If any of these three men are sentenced to prison, they must be kept at the United States Naval Station at Guantanamo Bay in Cuba. They must be held alone in their cells with no contact with other prisoners. They cannot have any contact with people from foreign countries. They can only receive mental health treatment if the medical staff at Guantanamo Bay specifically approves it. These three men cannot be moved to the main United States or to any other country.
